sources.title: Traditional Chinese Medicine: What You Need To Know
facts: Evidence differs by intervention; Chinese herbal product studies across many conditions have mixed or poor-quality results and do not support firm general conclusions.
TCM should not replace or delay conventional care, and herbal-product safety, contamination, substitution and interaction risks require clinician involvement.

sources.title: Global traditional medicine strategy 2025-2034
facts: WHO calls for strengthening the evidence base and for regulation that supports safe and effective traditional medicine.
Cultural or historical use is not, by itself, a safety, effectiveness or clinical-integration validation.

sources.title: Experimental Studies of Inter-Rater Agreement in Traditional Chinese Medicine: A Systematic Review
facts: Across 21 included studies, reported pairwise agreement averaged 57 percent and mean Cohen kappa averaged 0.34 in the subsets reporting those measures.
Agreement was generally low to moderate, with substantial methodological and reporting limitations.

sources.title: A review of the reliability of traditional East Asian medicine diagnoses
facts: Tongue, pulse and other traditional diagnostic variables showed considerable variability across studies.
Pattern diagnosis and treatment studies generally did not show high reliability.

sources.title: Reliability of Manual Pulse Diagnosis Methods in Traditional East Asian Medicine
facts: The review found little evidence for reliability of pulse diagnosis overall.
Acceptable reliability depended on concrete operational definitions; classical terminology and imprecise descriptions contributed to poor agreement.

sources.title: Development and evaluation of short-form version of the Constitution in Chinese Medicine Questionnaire
facts: The reference CCMQ contains 60 items and measures nine traditional constitution types.
A three-type prose classification is not equivalent to the CCMQ instrument or its scoring contract.

sources.title: Seasonal Flu Vaccine Basics
facts: CDC recommends seasonal influenza vaccination for everyone six months and older with rare exceptions.
The comparator does not support presenting Banlangen or Isatis leaves as established influenza prevention.

sources.title: Evaluating traditional Chinese medicine diagnostic instruments for functional dyspepsia
facts: Five identified pattern-diagnosis instruments had inadequate development quality.
None was considered reliable or valid for guiding TCM pattern diagnosis in that review.

boundaries: These comparators assess evidence, reliability, standardization and safety boundaries; they do not erase the historical or pedagogical value of traditional terminology.

A traditional framework statement may be taught as attributed tradition but must not be restated as modern anatomy, etiology, diagnosis, prognosis, prevention or treatment fact without separate evidence.
No finding in this layer makes a diagnosis, treatment, dosage, constitution score or emergency action eligible for product consumption.
